steps.title: Enter the first two years
steps.content: At $x=0$, the house is worth $174{,}000$. A $2.4\%$ yearly increase means multiply by $1.024$, so after one year the value is $174{,}000(1.024)=178{,}176$.

steps.title: Run exponential regression
steps.content: Use $y_1 \sim a b^{x_1}$. Desmos returns $a=174000$ and $b=1.024$, so the model is $y=174000(1.024)^x$.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: Choices A and B use multipliers less than $1$, so they model decreases. Choice D uses $1.24$, which is a $24\%$ increase. Choice C uses the regression multiplier $1.024$, so choice C is correct.
